Name: adaptive_routing_mixed_precision
Title: Dynamic Adaptive Routing for Mixed-Precision Quantization in Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: Can dynamic adaptive routing of mixed-precision quantization schemes enhance inference efficiency and accuracy in machine learning models while adhering to strict latency constraints?
Related Work: While existing works on mixed-precision quantization, such as SNIP and EMQ, focus on static configurations or heuristic optimizations, this proposal introduces a framework for dynamic adaptation during inference based on real-time performance metrics. Unlike static methods, this approach will allow models to adjust their precision dynamically, thus addressing the issues of performance degradation and latency overruns in varying operational environments.
Abstract: This proposal investigates a novel framework for dynamic adaptive routing of mixed-precision quantization schemes to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of machine learning models during inference. By leveraging real-time performance metrics, the proposed method will enable models to select optimal precision levels on-the-fly, thus minimizing latency and maximizing throughput. This exploration will build upon existing frameworks for mixed-precision quantization and introduce an adaptive routing mechanism based on a dynamically calibrated error budget. The framework will be validated through a series of experiments, contrasting performance with traditional static methods. We aim to demonstrate that the proposed adaptive routing significantly improves inference performance on latency-sensitive applications, leading to a new paradigm in efficient machine learning inference while adhering strictly to latency and resource constraints.
Experiments: Implement a baseline mixed-precision quantization model and an adaptive routing model.
Conduct a series of inference tests under varying load conditions to evaluate the adaptive routing mechanism's effectiveness in optimizing precision levels.
Measure performance metrics including inference time, accuracy, and resource utilization (memory and computational load).
Perform ablation studies to isolate the impact of adaptive routing versus static quantization methods.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential overhead from dynamic routing decision-making may offset benefits if not optimized properly.
The effectiveness of the adaptive routing mechanism may vary across different model architectures and tasks.
Real-time performance metrics may introduce noise that could lead to suboptimal routing decisions.
Implementation complexity may increase, potentially impacting reproducibility.
